title: unspecialize asserts !PyErr_Occurred() but LOAD_GLOBAL specialization leaves a MemoryError pending under OOM — Python/specialize.c:378
description: specialize_load_global_lock_held computes a dict keys-version (_PyDict_GetKeysVersionForCurrentState) that can set a MemoryError and return 0 under OOM; the code treats 0 as a benign backoff and goto fail -> unspecialize(instr), whose `assert(!PyErr_Occurred())` then aborts on debug builds.
notes: Assertion `!PyErr_Occurred()' in unspecialize. Specialization is supposed to be exception-neutral; _PyDict_GetKeysVersionForCurrentState returning 0 under OOM leaves a MemoryError that survives into the unspecialize backoff. Debug-only assert (NDEBUG strips it; release builds de-opt with a stray exception). Exception-state-under-OOM family (cf OOM-0008/0010/0011/0015); distinct from OOM-0011 (specialize LoadAttr at specialize.c:364). Fix: make the keys-version helper exception-neutral, or PyErr_Clear() before unspecialize. Minimization DONE (2026-06-19): deterministic stdlib reduction in repro.py, shrinkray-reduced from the sys-fuzz vehicle and re-verified 20/20. Load-bearing: func(*args) (a specialized CALL, not func()) and a bare undefined global in the finally (a LOAD_GLOBAL specialized at the crash; the not-found lookup forces both keys-versions to be computed -- the alloc that fails under OOM -- and leaves an exception pending). A defined name (raise RuntimeError) does not reproduce.
